danger_signals.visual_form: player sprite color contrast (opposing team color)
danger_signals.meaning: threat to ball control / scoring opportunity vulnerability

danger_signals.visual_form: goalie sprite depth in goal crease

safety_signals.visual_form: puck possession highlight / selected player sprite emphasis
empty rink area with no opposing player sprites
safety_signals.meaning: opportunity for safe passing / movement corridor

safety_signals.visual_form: goalie sprite active animation in crease

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: opposing team uses distinct contrasting jersey color
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: crowd coloration / rink board advertising reflects home team

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: team logo on away jersey sprites
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: visually distinct from home team through color separation
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: same rink environment but crowd context shifts based on game mode

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: team-specific organ music visual cue / scoreboard reset display
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: players spawn in center ice formation; gameplay reset to neutral position

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: scoreboard updates; goal horn/celebration animation trigger
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: celebrating team player sprite animations; crowd meter response; team-specific organ song plays (e.g. 'Brass Bonanza' for Hartford Whalers)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: penalized player sprite isolation / visual highlighting
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: player removed to penalty box; team plays with reduced player count; power play/penalty kill state established

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: camera/view emphasis on goalie and single attacker sprite
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: one-on-one player control; goalie manual control engaged; reduced ice participation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: bracket display in menu; game series counter (Best of Seven indicator)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: cumulative win tracking across multiple games; series elimination mechanics enforced

environmental_storytelling.example: Montreal Canadiens play 'Halte-là! Les Canadiens sont là!' at period start; Chicago Blackhawks play 'Here Come the Hawks'; St. Louis Blues play 'When the Saints Go Marching In' — creates faction identity through licensed authentic arena music
rosters reflect 1992-93 NHL season; includes new expansion teams (Mighty Ducks of Anaheim, Florida Panthers) — communicates real-world league composition and timeline authenticity
maintains distinct strategic visual language from side-view hockey games; communicates authentic broadcast perspective
one-timer move animation (player shoots directly off pass) — visual communication of authentic hockey mechanics

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: animation emphasizes control; movement speed reflects player attribute values (speed stat)
collision animation triggers; sprite interaction animation shows physical contact
animation shows save motion; positioning in crease; manual control responsiveness
displaced from crease position; animation shows out-of-position state
sprite velocity animation reflects pass/shot force; physical interaction with player sticks
visual feedback escalates; audio intensity increases
sprite isolated from play area; visual removal communicates reduced team capacity

notes: NHL '94 uses vertical camera perspective for authentic broadcast simulation. Visual communication emphasizes team differentiation through jersey color contrast, authentic league rosters (1992-93 season), and team-specific audio branding through licensed organ songs. Danger/safety signals primarily manifest through sprite positioning, animation frames, and possession states rather than explicit HUD warnings. World-state changes communicated through scoreboard updates, goal animations, and arena audio cues. The game's visual language prioritizes authenticity to televised NHL broadcasts and real player attributes (reflected in animation speed/style variation). Manual goalie control introduced new visual communication channel for defensive state. Faction identity relies heavily on jersey color and authentic team rosters rather than symbolic visual markers.
